<unordered_list><list_item><loc_68><loc_304><loc_429><loc_313>A. The value of R$_{GDJA}$ is measured with the device mounted on 1in 2$^{th}$ FR-board with 2oz. Copper, in a still air environment with T$_{A}$ =25° C. The value in any given application depends on the user's specific board design.</list_item>
<list_item><loc_68><loc_313><loc_347><loc_318>B. The power dissipation P$_{D}$ is based on T$_{J(MAX)}$=150° C, using ≤ 10s junction-to-ambient thermal resistance.</list_item>
<list_item><loc_68><loc_318><loc_428><loc_328>C. Repetitive rating, pulse width limited by junction temperature T$_{J(MAX)}$=150° C. Ratings are based on low frequency and duty cycles to keep initialT$_{J}$=25° C.</list_item>
<list_item><loc_68><loc_328><loc_312><loc_333>D. The R$_{GDJA}$ is the sum of the thermal impedance from junction to lead R$_{ML}$ and lead to ambient.</list_item>
<list_item><loc_68><loc_333><loc_342><loc_338>E. The static characteristics in Figures 1 to 6 are obtained using <300µs pulses, duty cycle 0.5% max.</list_item>
<list_item><loc_68><loc_338><loc_429><loc_347>F. These curves are based on the junction-to-ambient thermal impedance which is measured with the device mounted on 1in 2$^{th}$ FR-board with 2oz. Copper, assuming a maximum junction temperature of T$_{J(MAX)}$=150° C. The SOA curve provides a single pulse rating.</list_item>
</unordered_list>
